full_text:
Heatwave grips Rajasthan:Temperatures hit 42°C, hot winds likely in 6 cities including Jaipur today
- Copy link
Rajasthan is witnessing intense heat, with daytime temperatures rising sharply across the state. On Sunday, Churu, Chittorgarh and Kota recorded temperatures above 42°C. In more than 10 cities, the mercury ranged between 40°C and 41°C. In Kota, water tankers were used to spray roads to reduce heat.
The India Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for heatwave conditions in six districts, including Jaipur, for Monday. Hot winds are expected in Sri Ganganagar and Hanumangarh over the next few days.
Cloud cover was seen in parts of northern Rajasthan on Sunday, but it did not bring relief from the heat. Most parts of the state reported clear skies and strong sunlight. Heatwave conditions were observed in Jhunjhunu, Bharatpur, Alwar and Dholpur.
The Meteorological Centre in Jaipur has warned of continued heatwave conditions for the next three to four days across the state.
City-wise update
Jaipur: The capital recorded a maximum temperature of 40.5°C on Sunday, crossing 40°C for the second consecutive day. Night temperatures remained high at 27.5°C. A slight dip in temperature is expected on Monday.
Jodhpur: Jodhpur recorded a maximum temperature of 40°C, 1.2°C above normal. The minimum temperature was 26.4°C, nearly 3°C above normal. Clear skies and strong sunlight reduced daytime movement.
Kota: The maximum temperature stood at 42.1°C, slightly lower than Saturday’s 42.2°C. Hot winds continued through the day, affecting normal activity.
Alwar: A heatwave was recorded on Sunday, with the temperature reaching 41.5°C. Authorities have issued a four-day heatwave warning. Movement remained limited during peak hours.
Udaipur: Udaipur recorded 40.5°C. Shops in market areas installed coverings to block direct sunlight during the afternoon.
Ajmer: Ajmer recorded a maximum temperature of 38.8°C and a minimum of 22.4°C. Markets and highways saw reduced activity during the day. Temperatures are expected to rise further over the next two to three days.
Sikar: In Sikar, the maximum temperature crossed 40°C, reaching 40.5°C in Fatehpur. A steady rise in temperature has been observed over the past few days, with a large gap between day and night temperatures.
